channel.title: Screen Share
channel.description: A fortnightly film and television publication. Taste-led, independent, ad-free. One theme, six sections, the films and television worth your time.

item.title: Issue 003: Truth
item.dc:creator: Screen Share
item.description:
What does it take to actually know something?

Issue 003 explores the theme of Truth — through Errol Morris’s The Thin Blue Line, Oliver Stone’s JFK, and the films and television that prove truth is never simple to find — or to hold.

This issue includes: The Rewind, The Argument, The Signal, Discover by Mood, The Pulse, On Screen (TV), Flashpoint, and The Continent.

Read Issue 003 →

item.title: Issue 002: Power
item.dc:creator: Screen Share
item.description:
Who has it. Who loses it. What it costs.

Issue 002 explores the theme of Power — the mechanics of it, not the spectacle. From Sidney Lumet’s Network to Jonathan Glazer’s The Zone of Interest, from The Godfather to Gomorrah.

This issue includes: The Rewind, The Argument, The Signal (new), Discover by Mood, Weekly Pulse, On Screen (TV), and Flashpoint.

Read Issue 002 →

item.title: Issue 001: Hello
item.dc:creator: Screen Share
item.description:
First encounters, the moment of contact, and what we risk by reaching out.

Issue 001 explores the theme of Hello — the first contact, the outstretched hand, the moment before everything changes. From Denis Villeneuve's Arrival to Tarkovsky's Solaris, from the alien encounter to the human one.

This issue includes: The Rewind, The Argument, Discover by Mood, Weekly Pulse, On Screen (TV), Flashpoint, and Behind the Curtain.

Read Issue 001 →
